2026-04-07T03:29:21.3899270Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-07T03:29:21.3899741Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-07T03:29:21.3900136Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-07T03:29:21.3900454Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The package provides declarative provisioning support for 15+ Azure Monitor resource types including `ActionGroup`, `ActivityLogAlert`, `AlertRule`, `AutoscaleSetting`, `DataCollectionEndpoint`, `DataCollectionRule`, `DataCollectionRuleAssociation`, `DiagnosticSetting`, `LogProfile`, `MetricAlert`, `MonitorPrivateEndpointConnection`, `MonitorPrivateLinkScope`, `MonitorPrivateLinkScoped`, `MonitorWorkspace`, `PipelineGroup`, and `ScheduledQueryRule`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **\\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; subsection is empty** — the section header exists under \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34; but has no content\\n- **\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section is empty** — the section header exists but has no content\\n- **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section lacks Monitor-specific context** — only describes the general provisioning pattern without mentioning any of the specific resource types available in this package\\n- **Only one of 15+ resource types is demonstrated** — only `ActionGroup`+`ActivityLogAlert` have examples; high-value types like `DiagnosticSetting`, `MetricAlert`, `DataCollectionRule`, and `ScheduledQueryRule` have no examples at all\\n- **Snippet region is in a non-standard location** — the `#region Snippet:MonitorServiceHealthAlert` is inside a `Trycep.Define()` callback in `BasicTests.cs`, not in a dedicated `tests/Samples/` file following the standard sample pattern\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/monitor/Azure.Provisioning.Monitor/README.md` — Edit\\n\\nFill in the two empty sections and expand Key concepts:\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section** — Replace the empty section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\nThis library generates declarative Bicep/ARM infrastructure templates and does not connect to Azure services directly. Authentication for **deployment** is handled by the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) or `Azure.ResourceManager`. No client credentials are required to define your infrastructure in code.\\n\\nIf you need to deploy programmatically, authenticate using `DefaultAzureCredential` from [Azure.Identity]((www.nuget.org/redacted)\\n\\n```\\ndotnet add package Azure.Identity\\n```\\n\\n```\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\n\\nArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section** — Replace the existing paragraph with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.Monitor` lets you define Azure Monitor infrastructure in C# and generate Bicep or ARM templates. It covers:\\n\\n- **Alerts** — `ActivityLogAlert`, `AlertRule`, `MetricAlert`, `ScheduledQueryRule`\\n- **Action Groups** — `ActionGroup` for routing notifications\\n- **Data Collection** — `DataCollectionRule`, `DataCollectionEndpoint`, `DataCollectionRuleAssociation`\\n- **Diagnostic Settings** — `DiagnosticSetting` to forward logs/metrics to storage, Event Hubs, or Log Analytics\\n- **Autoscale** — `AutoscaleSetting` for resource scaling rules\\n- **Monitor Workspaces** — `MonitorWorkspace` for Azure Monitor managed Prometheus\\n- **Private Link** — `MonitorPrivateLinkScope`, `MonitorPrivateEndpointConnection`, `MonitorPrivateLinkScoped`\\n- **Pipeline** — `PipelineGroup`, `LogProfile`\\n\\nAll resources are designed for use with the `Infrastructure` class from [Azure.Provisioning]((www.nuget.org/redacted), and deploy using `azd` or the Azure.ResourceManager SDK.\\n```\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section** — Replace the empty section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Explore the [Azure Monitor documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Learn about [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for deploying generated Bicep templates\\n- Browse the [Azure Provisioning samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/provisioning)\\n- Review the full list of provisionable resources in the [API reference]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/monitor/Azure.Provisioning.Monitor/tests/Samples/Sample1_MonitorResources.cs` — Create\\n\\nCreate this new file to add snippet-backed examples for key resource types:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Provisioning.Expressions;\\nusing Azure.Provisioning.Resources;\\nusing CreateDiagnosticSetting()\\n    {\\n        await using Trycep test = new Trycep().Define(\\n            ctx =\\u0026gt;\\n            {\\n                #region Snippet:MonitorDiagnosticSetting\\n new(nameof(resourceId), typeof(string))\\n                    {\\n                        Description = \\u0026#34;The resource ID to configure diagnostics for.\\u0026#34;\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(resourceId);\\n\\n                ProvisioningParameter workspaceId =\\n                    new(nameof(workspaceId), typeof(string))\\n                    {\\n                        Description = \\u0026#34;Log Analytics workspace resource ID.\\u0026#34;\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(workspaceId);\\n\\n                DiagnosticSetting diagnostics return infra;\\n            });\\n        // Test validates Bicep output\\n        _ = test;\\n    }\\n\\n    [Test]\\n    public async Task CreateDataCollectionRule()\\n    {\\n        await using Trycep test = new Trycep().Define(\\n            ctx =\\u0026gt;\\n            {\\n                #region Snippet:MonitorDataCollectionRule\\n                Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n                DataCollectionEndpoint endpoint =\\n                    new(nameof(endpoint), DataCollectionEndpoint.ResourceVersions.V2023_03_11)\\n                    {\\n                        Location = new AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;),\\n                        NetworkAclsPublicNetworkAccess = KnownPublicNetworkAccess.Enabled\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(endpoint);\\n\\n                DataCollectionRule rule =\\n                    new(nameof(rule), DataCollectionRule.ResourceVersions.V2023_03_11)\\n                    {\\n                        Location = new AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;),\\n                        DataCollectionEndpointId = endpoint.Id\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(rule);\\n The package provides declarative provisioning support for 15+ Azure Monitor resource types including `ActionGroup`, `ActivityLogAlert`, `AlertRule`, `AutoscaleSetting`, `DataCollectionEndpoint`, `DataCollectionRule`, `DataCollectionRuleAssociation`, `DiagnosticSetting`, `LogProfile`, `MetricAlert`, `MonitorPrivateEndpointConnection`, `MonitorPrivateLinkScope`, `MonitorPrivateLinkScoped`, `MonitorWorkspace`, `PipelineGroup`, and `ScheduledQueryRule`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **\\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; subsection is empty** — the section header exists under \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34; but has no content\\n- **\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section is empty** — the section header exists but has no content\\n- **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section lacks Monitor-specific context** — only describes the general provisioning pattern without mentioning any of the specific resource types available in this package\\n- **Only one of 15+ resource types is demonstrated** — only `ActionGroup`+`ActivityLogAlert` have examples; high-value types like `DiagnosticSetting`, `MetricAlert`, `DataCollectionRule`, and `ScheduledQueryRule` have no examples at all\\n- **Snippet region is in a non-standard location** — the `#region Snippet:MonitorServiceHealthAlert` is inside a `Trycep.Define()` callback in `BasicTests.cs`, not in a dedicated `tests/Samples/` file following the standard sample pattern\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/monitor/Azure.Provisioning.Monitor/README.md` — Edit\\n\\nFill in the two empty sections and expand Key concepts:\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section** — Replace the empty section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\nThis library generates declarative Bicep/ARM infrastructure templates and does not connect to Azure services directly. Authentication for **deployment** is handled by the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) or `Azure.ResourceManager`. No client credentials are required to define your infrastructure in code.\\n\\nIf you need to deploy programmatically, authenticate using `DefaultAzureCredential` from [Azure.Identity]((www.nuget.org/redacted)\\n\\n```\\ndotnet add package Azure.Identity\\n```\\n\\n```\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\n\\nArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section** — Replace the existing paragraph with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.Monitor` lets you define Azure Monitor infrastructure in C# and generate Bicep or ARM templates. It covers:\\n\\n- **Alerts** — `ActivityLogAlert`, `AlertRule`, `MetricAlert`, `ScheduledQueryRule`\\n- **Action Groups** — `ActionGroup` for routing notifications\\n- **Data Collection** — `DataCollectionRule`, `DataCollectionEndpoint`, `DataCollectionRuleAssociation`\\n- **Diagnostic Settings** — `DiagnosticSetting` to forward logs/metrics to storage, Event Hubs, or Log Analytics\\n- **Autoscale** — `AutoscaleSetting` for resource scaling rules\\n- **Monitor Workspaces** — `MonitorWorkspace` for Azure Monitor managed Prometheus\\n- **Private Link** — `MonitorPrivateLinkScope`, `MonitorPrivateEndpointConnection`, `MonitorPrivateLinkScoped`\\n- **Pipeline** — `PipelineGroup`, `LogProfile`\\n\\nAll resources are designed for use with the `Infrastructure` class from [Azure.Provisioning]((www.nuget.org/redacted), and deploy using `azd` or the Azure.ResourceManager SDK.\\n```\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section** — Replace the empty section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Explore the [Azure Monitor documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Learn about [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for deploying generated Bicep templates\\n- Browse the [Azure Provisioning samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/provisioning)\\n- Review the full list of provisionable resources in the [API reference]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/monitor/Azure.Provisioning.Monitor/tests/Samples/Sample1_MonitorResources.cs` — Create\\n\\nCreate this new file to add snippet-backed examples for key resource types:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. CreateDiagnosticSetting()\\n    {\\n        await using Trycep test = new Trycep().Define(\\n            ctx =\\u0026gt;\\n            {\\n                #region Snippet:MonitorDiagnosticSetting\\n                Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n                // Reference an existing resource to attach diagnostics to\\n                ProvisioningParameter resourceId =\\n                    new(nameof(resourceId), typeof(string))\\n                    {\\n                        Description = \\u0026#34;The resource ID to configure diagnostics for.\\u0026#34;\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(resourceId);\\n\\n                ProvisioningParameter workspaceId =\\n                    new(nameof(workspaceId), typeof(string))\\n                    {\\n                        Description = \\u0026#34;Log Analytics workspace resource ID.\\u0026#34;\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(workspaceId);\\n\\n                DiagnosticSetting diagnostics =\\n                    new(nameof(diagnostics))\\n                    {\\n                        Name = \\u0026#34;myDiagnosticSetting\\u0026#34;,\\n                        WorkspaceId = workspaceId,\\n                        Logs =\\n                        {\\n                            new LogSettings\\n                            {\\n                                CategoryGroup = \\u0026#34;allLogs\\u0026#34;,\\n                                IsEnabled = true\\n                            }\\n                        },\\n                        Metrics =\\n                        {\\n                            new MetricSettings\\n                            {\\n                                Category = \\u0026#34;AllMetrics\\u0026#34;,\\n                                IsEnabled = true\\n                            }\\n                        }\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(diagnostics);\\n                #endregion\\n\\n                return infra;\\n            });\\n        // Test validates Bicep output\\n        _ = test;\\n    }\\n\\n    [Test]\\n    public async Task CreateDataCollectionRule()\\n    {\\n        await using new AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;),\\n                        NetworkAclsPublicNetworkAccess = KnownPublicNetworkAccess.Enabled\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(endpoint);\\n\\n                DataCollectionRule rule =\\n                    new(nameof(rule), DataCollectionRule.ResourceVersions.V2023_03_11)\\n                    {\\n         #endregion\\n\\n                return infra;\\n            });\\n        _ = test;\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n#### File 3: `sdk/monitor/Azure.Provisioning.Monitor/README.md` — Edit (add snippet Users discovering the library had no way to know what Azure Monitor resources were available without reading the generated API. A table of all 16 `ProvisionableResource` types with brief descriptions has been added:\\n\\n| Resource | Description |\\n|---|---|\\n| `ActionGroup` | Defines a set of notification actions triggered by alerts |\\n| `ActivityLogAlert` | Creates alerts based on Azure activity log events |\\n| `AlertRule` | Classic metric alert rule for Azure Monitor metrics |\\n| `AutoscaleSetting` | Configures automatic scaling of compute resources |\\n| `DataCollectionEndpoint` | Defines an endpoint for data collection pipelines |\\n| `DataCollectionRule` | Specifies how data is collected and routed to destinations |\\n| `DataCollectionRuleAssociation` | Associates a data collection rule with a monitored resource |\\n| `DiagnosticSetting` | Routes diagnostic logs and metrics to storage, event hubs, or Log Analytics |\\n| `LogProfile` | Classic log profile for routing activity logs (legacy) |\\n| `MetricAlert` | Creates alerts based on Azure Monitor metric thresholds or dynamic conditions |\\n| `MonitorPrivateEndpointConnection` | Manages private endpoint connections for Azure Monitor Private Link |\\n| `MonitorPrivateLinkScope` | Defines the scope of a Private Link configuration for Azure Monitor |\\n| `MonitorPrivateLinkScoped` | Associates resources with a Monitor Private Link scope |\\n| `MonitorWorkspace` | Azure Monitor workspace for storing Prometheus metrics |\\n| `PipelineGroup` | Groups data collection pipelines for centralized management |\\n| `ScheduledQueryRule` | Creates alerts based on Log Analytics or metrics queries |\\n\\n**Next steps — useful links**\\n\\nThe `## Next steps` section was entirely empty. Three links were added:\\n- Azure Monitor overview documentation\\n- Azure Developer CLI getting-started guide (for `azd` deployments)\\n- Azure Monitor quickstart templates on GitHub\\n\\n### No behavior changes\\n\\nDocumentation-only change; no code was modified.\\n\\n### Reviewers\\n\\ncc `@ArcturusZhang` `@ArthurMa1978` `@m-nash` (Azure.Provisioning CODEOWNERS)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u003e Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/23939790158/agentic_workflow) · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+%22gh-aw-workflow-id%3A+update-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026type=pullrequests)\\n\\n\\u003c!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: Update Docs, engine: copilot, model: auto, id: 23939790158, workflow_id: update-samples-and-docs, run: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/23939790158 --\\u003e\\n\\n\\u003c!-- gh-aw-workflow-id: update-samples-and-docs --\\u003e\\n\\n---\\n\\n\\u003e [!NOTE]\\n\\u003e This was originally intended as a pull request, but GitHub Actions is not permitted to create or approve pull requests in this repository.\\n\\u003e The changes have been pushed to branch `docs/azure-provisioning-monitor-readme-improvements-386d100ba96aaa09`.\\n\\u003e\\n\\u003e **[Click here to create the pull request](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/compare/main...docs/azure-provisioning-monitor-readme-improvements-386d100ba96aaa09?expand=1\\u0026title=docs%3A%20improve%20Azure.Provisioning.Monitor%20README%20with%20resource%20types%20and%20next%20steps)**\\n\\nTo fix the permissions issue, go to **Settings** → **Actions** → **General** and enable **Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests**. You can then use azd to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\\n \\n+The following Azure Monitor resource types are supported:\\n+\\n+| Resource | Description |\\n+|---|---|\\n+| `ActionGroup` | Defines a set of notification actions triggered by alerts |\\n+| `ActivityLogAlert` | Creates alerts based on Azure activity log events (e.g., service health, resource changes) |\\n+| `AlertRule` | Classic metric alert rule for Azure Monitor metrics |\\n+| `AutoscaleSetting` | Configures automatic scaling of compute resources based on metrics |\\n+| `DataCollectionEndpoint` | Defines an endpoint for data collection pipelines |\\n+| `DataCollectionRule` | Specifies how data is collected a
